AdminMenu.description:
The complete administrative menu tree.

Includes hidden categories/items (this is the management view); M3A
carries no modifier or media data (they arrive with M3B/M3C).
AdminMenu.title: AdminMenu

AuditEventPage.description: Cursor page (``id DESC``); ``next_before_id`` feeds the next request.
AuditEventPage.title: AuditEventPage

BusinessCreate.description: Platform command to create a business (starts in provisioning).
BusinessCreate.title: BusinessCreate

BusinessPage.description: A bounded page of businesses for the platform catalog.
BusinessPage.title: BusinessPage

BusinessSummary.description: Public representation of a business (never the ORM object).
BusinessSummary.title: BusinessSummary

CategoryCreate.description: Create a menu category (appended at the end of the menu).
CategoryCreate.title: CategoryCreate

CategoryReorder.description: Full-set category reorder: every category id, in the new order.
CategoryReorder.title: CategoryReorder

CategoryUpdate.description: Partial category update; only supplied fields change.
CategoryUpdate.title: CategoryUpdate

CategoryWithItems.description: A category and its items in position order (hidden included).
CategoryWithItems.title: CategoryWithItems

DeletedResponse.description:
Explicit confirmation body for delete commands (the M2D
``InvitationRevokedResponse`` pattern — commands return a clear result,
blueprint §10.4).
DeletedResponse.title: DeletedResponse

EmptyCommand.description:
Body for no-argument lifecycle commands.

Present so an unexpected JSON field is rejected (422) rather than
silently ignored (approved amendment 3): a lifecycle POST carries no
data, but it must still be strict about what it accepts.
EmptyCommand.title: EmptyCommand

EntitlementSet.description:
Platform command: the complete desired feature set (idempotent).

Unknown keys fail schema validation (422) — the registry is the closed
value set.
EntitlementSet.title: EntitlementSet

EntitlementsResponse.description: The effective (recognized, enabled) feature set of a business.
EntitlementsResponse.title: EntitlementsResponse

FeatureKey.description:
Platform product features assignable to a business (append-only).

Seeded with exactly ``online_ordering`` (approved M2 decision); its
enforcement arrives with checkout (M6). Catalog/storefront, SMS,
Facebook publishing, and the AI assistant register here in their own
milestones.
FeatureKey.title: FeatureKey

InvitationAcceptExistingRequest.description: Authenticated acceptance adding a membership to the caller.
InvitationAcceptExistingRequest.title: InvitationAcceptExistingRequest

InvitationAcceptRequest.description: Public acceptance creating a new account (no auto-login).
InvitationAcceptRequest.title: InvitationAcceptRequest

InvitationCreate.description: Command to invite a member (business-scoped or platform route).
InvitationCreate.title: InvitationCreate

InvitationIssueResponse.description:
One-time issuance result (ADR-014).

``token`` is the raw secret, returned exactly once to the authorized
issuer for out-of-band delivery — never a URL, never stored, never
logged, never shown again.
InvitationIssueResponse.title: InvitationIssueResponse

InvitationPreviewRequest.description: Public preview lookup — the token travels in the body, never a URL.
InvitationPreviewRequest.title: InvitationPreviewRequest

InvitationSummary.description: One pending invitation (operational list; no token, no hash).
InvitationSummary.title: InvitationSummary

ItemAvailabilitySet.description: The availability command body ("sold out today" toggle).
ItemAvailabilitySet.title: ItemAvailabilitySet

ItemCreate.description:
Create a menu item (appended at the end of its category).

New items start available, not hidden, and not featured; those states
change through PATCH and the availability command.
ItemCreate.title: ItemCreate

ItemImageSet.description:
The item-image command body (M3C): attach/replace/clear + alt text.

Both fields are genuinely nullable: ``media_id`` null clears the image;
``alt_text`` null removes the description. Alt text without an image is
rejected (the database also enforces this). Empty/whitespace alt text
normalizes to null.
ItemImageSet.title: ItemImageSet

ItemReorder.description: Full-set item reorder within one category.
ItemReorder.title: ItemReorder

ItemSummary.description:
One menu item (administrative projection).

``price_minor`` is integer minor units; the currency is the business's
own (``businesses.currency``) and is deliberately not repeated here.
ItemSummary.title: ItemSummary

ItemUpdate.description:
Partial item update; only supplied fields change.

``category_id`` moves the item (appended at the end of the destination
category). ``is_available`` is deliberately absent: availability is the
separate staff-reachable command (ruling D4), never part of this PATCH.
ItemUpdate.title: ItemUpdate

MediaAssetPage.description: One page of a business's media assets (limit/offset, newest first).
MediaAssetPage.title: MediaAssetPage

MediaAssetView.description:
One media asset (administrative projection).

Deliberately excludes ``checksum_sha256`` and every storage key/path:
those are internal storage metadata (R3/R4). ``pending_expires_at`` is
populated only for pending assets.
MediaAssetView.title: MediaAssetView

MediaDeletedResponse.description: Explicit confirmation body for the media delete command.
MediaDeletedResponse.title: MediaDeletedResponse

MediaVariantView.description: One generated responsive rendition (no key, no checksum).
MediaVariantView.title: MediaVariantView

MembershipSummary.description: One of the caller's business memberships (composed projection).
MembershipSummary.title: MembershipSummary

ModifierGroupCreate.description:
Create a modifier group (appended at the end of the item's groups).

A group may be created with zero options: satisfiability is computed
and report-only (D5). ``max_select`` null means unlimited.
ModifierGroupCreate.title: ModifierGroupCreate

ModifierGroupReorder.description: Full-set group reorder: every group id of the item, in order.
ModifierGroupReorder.title: ModifierGroupReorder

ModifierGroupUpdate.description:
Partial group update; only supplied fields change.

``max_select`` is the one genuinely nullable field: an explicit null
sets the group to unlimited (the description-clearing pattern). When
only one side of the min/max pair is supplied, the service validates
the effective pair against the stored values.
ModifierGroupUpdate.title: ModifierGroupUpdate

ModifierGroupView.description:
One group with its ordered options and computed satisfiability.

``active_option_count`` and ``is_satisfiable`` are computed from the
authoritative post-mutation state (D5) — never stored.
ModifierGroupView.title: ModifierGroupView

ModifierGroupsView.description: The bounded per-item modifier tree (D2): ordered groups.
ModifierGroupsView.title: ModifierGroupsView

ModifierOptionReorder.description: Full-set option reorder within one group.
ModifierOptionReorder.title: ModifierOptionReorder

ModifierOptionUpdate.description: Partial option update; availability rides this PATCH (D3).
ModifierOptionUpdate.title: ModifierOptionUpdate

PasswordResetIssueRequest.description: Platform command: issue a reset token for an account (M2D).
PasswordResetIssueRequest.title: PasswordResetIssueRequest

PasswordResetIssueResponse.description:
One-time issuance result (ADR-014).

``token`` is the raw secret, returned exactly once to the authorized
issuer for out-of-band delivery — never a URL, never stored, never
logged, never shown again.
PasswordResetIssueResponse.title: PasswordResetIssueResponse

PasswordResetRedeemRequest.description: Public command: redeem a reset token and set a new password (M2D).
PasswordResetRedeemRequest.title: PasswordResetRedeemRequest

PublicMenu.description:
The complete public menu of the host-resolved Business.

`business` is the sole source of name, slug, timezone, and currency.
`featured_item_ids` lists at most the featured-policy maximum and
refers only to items present in `categories`.
PublicMenu.title: PublicMenu

PublicMenuCategory.description: One visible menu section with at least one publicly visible item.
PublicMenuCategory.title: PublicMenuCategory

PublicMenuImage.description:
The item's image: canonical dimensions plus responsive renditions.

Carries no asset id, storage key, path, or checksum — the URL is the
resource identity (ADR-017 R3). `alt_text` is the contextual alt text
of this attachment, not a property of the asset, and may be null.
PublicMenuImage.title: PublicMenuImage

PublicMenuImageVariant.description:
One responsive rendition, with its true pixel dimensions.

The client selects a rendition (``srcset``); the API publishes every
one it has. `url` is relative — the storefront is served same-origin
with the tenant host (ADR-013).
PublicMenuImageVariant.title: PublicMenuImageVariant

PublicMenuItem.description:
One publicly visible menu item.

`is_available` is the "sold out today" state; a sold-out item stays
listed. `is_orderable` is computed: available **and** every required
modifier group currently satisfiable. M6 remains authoritative at
order time — these are display facts, not a checkout guarantee.
PublicMenuItem.title: PublicMenuItem

PublicModifierGroup.description:
One customization group a guest can currently complete.

Only satisfiable groups are projected, so `is_satisfiable` and
`active_option_count` (administrative diagnostics) are absent —
`options` is exactly the selectable set. `max_select` null means
unlimited by configuration.
PublicModifierGroup.title: PublicModifierGroup

PublicModifierOption.description:
One selectable option (available options only).

Unavailable options are omitted from the projection entirely, so no
availability flag is needed: everything present is selectable.
PublicModifierOption.title: PublicModifierOption

PublicSiteSummary.description:
Minimal public projection returned for a resolved active business
(M2C, ADR-013).

Deliberately narrow: a 200 already proves the business is active, so no
``status`` field is needed, and no id/timestamps/management data are
exposed on the unauthenticated surface.
PublicSiteSummary.title: PublicSiteSummary

SessionResponse.description: Current authenticated identity plus the CSRF synchronizer token.
SessionResponse.title: SessionResponse

SessionView.description:
Current identity, CSRF token, and the caller's memberships.

Superset of the login ``SessionResponse``: adds ``memberships``.
Platform admins hold no memberships, so their list is empty.
SessionView.title: SessionView

post.description: Authenticate and open a fresh session (sets the session cookie).
Revoke the current session and clear the cookie.

get.description: Current identity, CSRF token, and the caller's business memberships.
Read the caller's own business (requires membership).

This business's audit trail (owner/manager via ``business.audit.read``;
platform-level events are structurally excluded).

post.description: Create a category (appended at the end of the menu).
Full-set category reorder; returns the updated menu tree.

delete.description: Delete an empty category (non-empty → 409, ruling D7).

patch.description: Update a category's name, description, or visibility.

post.description: Create an item (appended at the end of its category).
Full-set item reorder within one category; returns the menu tree.

delete.description: Delete an item; remaining positions renormalize.

get.description: Read one item (any member).

patch.description:
Update an item (name/description/price/hidden/featured/tags/category).

Availability is deliberately not part of this PATCH — it is the
separate ``catalog_item_availability_set`` command (ruling D4).

post.description: The "sold out today" toggle (staff-reachable, ruling D4).

Attach/replace/clear the item's image and its contextual alt text
(M3C); exact no-ops change nothing. Attaching promotes the media asset
to active; an expired or cross-tenant media reference → 409.

get.description: The item's bounded modifier tree with computed satisfiability (D2).

post.description: Create a modifier group (appended; zero options is valid, D5).
Full-set group reorder; returns the item's modifier tree.

get.description: The complete administrative menu tree (hidden entries included).

delete.description: Delete a group; its options cascade; sibling positions compact.

patch.description:
Update a group's name or selection rules (explicit null max_select
means unlimited).

post.description: Create an option (appended, available); returns the parent group.
Full-set option reorder; returns the parent group.

delete.description:
Delete an option; returns the surviving parent group (possibly
unsatisfiable with zero active options).

patch.description:
Update an option (name/price delta/availability, D3); returns the
recomputed parent group.

get.description: The business's enabled features (M2D; any member may read).
Pending invitations (history lives in the audit trail).

post.description: Invite a member (role ceiling applies; token returned once).
Revoke a pending invitation (allowed in any business status).

get.description: A page of the business's media assets (newest first, any member).

post.description:
Upload one image; returns the stored asset (pending).

Auth and CSRF run as dependencies (before any body parse); the handler
then completes the pre-body gate (capability + non-locking lifecycle)
on the request session and streams the bounded body. Multipart
extraction, processing, object storage, and the authoritative
transaction all run in an AnyIO worker thread — only the bounded async
streaming stays on the event loop (final correction 2).

file.description: A single static JPEG, PNG, or WebP image.

delete.description: Delete an asset (referenced → 409); objects removed after commit.

get.description: One asset with its variants (any member; pending included).

Authorized admin preview of one stored object (pending allowed, R7).

Serves WebP with a fixed content type, ``nosniff``, a server-composed
inline filename, and ``no-store`` (public caching is the M3D/M4
decision). Missing canonical or variant → 404.

post.description: Create the invited account + membership (no auto-login).

Add the invited membership to the authenticated caller's account.

The supported path for one user to belong to multiple businesses; the
caller's email must match the invitation's.
Accept-page context: business name, role, and a masked email hint.
Redeem a reset token: set the password, revoke every session.

get.description: Platform-wide audit stream (``platform.audit.read``).
Bounded platform catalog page (created_at DESC, id DESC).

post.description: Create a business (starts in provisioning).

get.description: Platform read of any business.

post.description: provisioning → active (requires at least one owner).
suspended → closed (terminal).

put.description: Replace the business's feature set (idempotent; diff is audited).

get.description: Pending invitations for any business.

post.description: Invite a member of any role (token returned once).
Revoke any pending invitation (any business status).
suspended → active.
active → suspended.
Issue a single-use reset token for an account (returned once).

get.description:
Deliver one image of the Business resolved from the Host.

Serves the re-encoded WebP canonical rendition or one of its
responsive variants, addressed by opaque asset id and logical variant
name. Supports conditional requests through a strong derived ETag;
``Range`` is ignored and the complete representation is returned.

The public menu of the Business resolved from the Host.

Hidden items and invisible categories are excluded, categories with no
publicly visible item are omitted, and unavailable modifier options and
unsatisfiable groups are dropped. Prices are integer minor units in the
business's own currency.
Minimal public summary of the Business resolved from the Host.
Report that the process is up and able to serve HTTP.

Report whether essential dependencies are reachable.

The check is deliberately cheap (bounded-timeout SELECT 1). The process
starts even when the database is down and reports 503 here until it
recovers — readiness gates traffic, liveness gates restarts.

Not-ready is an error state, so it uses the ADR-008 envelope
(``dependency_unavailable``) with the failing checks preserved in
``error.details.checks``.
